outcome_contract.why: German and Italian visitors get a mixed-language experience — translated product pages but English navigation, consent banner, footer links, and trust strip. This undermines trust, especially on legal pages where language matters.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All persistent UI chrome (header, footer, consent banner, trust strip) displays in the visitor's selected language

engineering_coverage.applicable: DE/IT string values added to `CHROME_DEFAULTS` for all 4 chrome surfaces
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No component changes; translations live in `contentPacket.ts`

engineering_coverage.applicable: All DE/IT chrome surfaces correct after deploy; `resolveLocale` fallback to EN still works

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: No auth, session, or data exposure changes
Static strings, no audit trail needed

stage_payload.summary: All four Caryina chrome components (Header, SiteFooter, ConsentBanner, ShippingReturnsTrustBlock) are already locale-wired via `getChromeContent(lang)`. The bug is that `CHROME_EN_DEFAULTS` and the JSON chrome section both have only English values — no German or Italian translations exist anywhere. The fix is: (1) rename the constant to `CHROME_DEFAULTS`, add 50 new translation values (25 strings × 2 locales), and atomically remove the `chrome` key from `site-content.generated.json` so the enriched code constant becomes the active source; (2) add a focused unit test confirming locale-correct o
goals: Add DE/IT translations for all 25 chrome strings across 5 groups (header, footer, consent, trust, notifyMe)
Make `CHROME_DEFAULTS` the authoritative and durable source of truth for chrome translations
Remove the `chrome` key from the generated JSON to eliminate materializer durability risk
Add unit test coverage for `getChromeContent` locale resolution
non_goals: Translating product copy, policy body text, or SEO content
Adding new chrome components or changing chrome structure
Fixing the `productPage.trustStrip` materializer durability risk (separate concern, pre-existing)
constraints: TASK-01 code change + JSON chrome removal must be a single atomic commit (JSON chrome wins over code if only one is done)
Translations must be natural DE/IT — not machine-literal
assumptions: `LocalizedText` type already has `de?` and `it?` optional keys — no type changes needed
`cachedPayload` in `readPayload()` is process-level — no runtime cache invalidation required for deploy
selected_approach: What was chosen: Enrich `CHROME_EN_DEFAULTS` (renamed `CHROME_DEFAULTS`) in `contentPacket.ts` with `de`/`it` keys for all 27 strings; remove `chrome` key from `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` atomically
Why planning is not reopening option selection: Analysis conclusively eliminated Options B and C (materializer responsibility mismatch; unnecessary merge complexity). Option A is fully determined.

task_briefs.title: Add DE/IT translations to `CHROME_DEFAULTS` + remove JSON chrome

process_topology.note: None: no material process topology change The runtime chrome-read path is unchanged. After TASK-01 lands, `getChromeContent(lang)` reads from `CHROME_DEFAULTS` (code constant) instead of `payload.chrome` (JSON), and `localizedText(value, locale)` returns the correct DE/IT string for each field. No new flows, no new components, no operator runbook changes. ---
